operationalSituations.description: Normal operation — CAN bus active, frames being sent and received
Bus shutdown — close() called, subscribers draining

hazards.description: Malformed CAN frame transmitted to the bus (invalid ID, bad DLC, protocol constraint violated)
Safety-critical payload silently corrupted — corruption not detected by the E2E layer
Replayed or out-of-order E2E-protected message accepted as authentic
Subscriber channel overflow — frames silently dropped without application awareness
ISO-TP receive blocks indefinitely when consecutive frames do not arrive

safetyGoals.description: rust-CAN shall reject all frames that violate CAN protocol constraints before transmission
safetyGoals.safeState: Frame is rejected with InvalidFrame error; no transmission occurs

safetyGoals.description: rust-CAN E2E protection shall detect payload corruption and replay attacks
safetyGoals.safeState: Corrupted or replayed data is rejected with E2EError; payload is not forwarded to application

safetyGoals.description: rust-CAN shall bound all blocking receive operations by the caller-supplied context deadline
safetyGoals.safeState: Timeout returns ErrTimeout; caller can retry or escalate
